page: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation running at 2.0 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Memory capacity is 32.0 GB, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 960 GB local NVMe SSD, while no dedicated GPU hardware is present. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests and bzip3 single-threaded decompression. However, multi-core scaling shows some limitations, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores landing in the average tier, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 25%.

The memory subsystem performs strongly, with PassMark memory scores and large-block write bandwidth ranking in the top 25%, though read bandwidth remains average. For database and web workloads, the server exhibits some tradeoffs; static web serving throughput and PassMark database operations benchmarked in the bottom 25%, while Redis SET operations maintained average throughput. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads, making it suitable for caching layers, memory-intensive data processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with 4 shared vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes a 960 GB local NVMe SSD and lacks GPU acceleration. The server delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance, though multi-core scaling and database operations fall into average and lower tiers. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU and strong memory write bandwidth, this configuration provides a qualitatively cost-efficient resource profile for memory-intensive applications. It is well-suited for caching systems, memory-bound data processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring significant local storage capacity without needing dedicated CPU threads.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory write performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-4c-32gb-960s-amd features 4 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining AMD EPYC Genoa processing with a high-capacity local NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Genoa processor with 4 shared vCPUs and 32 MB L3 cache
32.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Bundled 960 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Strong memory write bandwidth and average Redis performance
Suited for caching layers, memory-bound processing, and local storage workloads
